faqs.q: How much does hot tub repair cost in Spokane?
faqs.a: Hot tub repair in Spokane typically ranges from $150 to $1,500 depending on the issue. Common repairs: heating element replacement ($200–$400), pump repair ($300–$600), control board replacement ($400–$800), jet replacement ($150–$300). Diagnostic visit is included with any repair.

faqs.q: My hot tub isn't heating — what's wrong?
faqs.a: The most common causes are a failed heating element, faulty thermostat, failed high-limit sensor, or a circulation pump issue. We diagnose the root cause before recommending any repair.

faqs.q: Do you service hot tubs in Spokane Valley and Liberty Lake?
faqs.a: Yes — we serve all of Spokane County including Spokane Valley, Liberty Lake, Cheney, Medical Lake, Airway Heights, and across the border into Post Falls and Coeur d'Alene, Idaho.

faqs.q: How quickly can you come out for a repair?
faqs.a: We typically schedule service visits within 24–48 hours. For urgent issues like a completely non-functioning hot tub before cold weather, we offer same-day service when available.

faqs.q: Do you work on all hot tub brands?
faqs.a: Yes — we service all major brands including Jacuzzi, Hot Spring, Sundance, Caldera, Bullfrog, Master Spa, Marquis, and most others. We stock common parts for the most popular brands.
